text: Does a KdV soliton survive when it collides with a Burgers bore in the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system? Transmit, reflect, fuse, or destroy?

ic: u(x,0)=1.5*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2 (smoothed bore); v(x,0)=1.5*sech^2(x+8) (KdV soliton at x=-8)
method: Fourier pseudospectral spatial (periodic, Nx=256) + explicit RK4 time; NO dealiasing, NO splitting, NO filtering — simplest baseline per progressive-complexity discipline
bank_use_rationale: NoKB condition — no bank available; E1 is the textbook simplest baseline (pseudospectral + RK4) appropriate for periodic problem with a third-order dispersion term that central FD cannot resolve cheaply; dt=1e-4 chosen from explicit RK4 stability on v_xxx (k_max^3 ~ 1.9e4, dt<2.8/k_max^3~1.5e-4)

diagnostics.interpretation: Steep bore (width 0.5) has substantial energy at all wavenumbers up to k_max; pseudospectral derivative of nonlinear products (3 v^2, u v) generates aliased high-k modes which cascade and overflow. Spectrum at k_max for u0 is ~0.003 — non-negligible. Dispersion CFL margin dt*k^3_max=1.93 is fine in isolation (<2.83), so primary failure is nonlinear aliasing, not linear stiffness.

rationale: Standard pseudospectral no-dealias failure mode on shocks/bores; diagnostic clearly points to aliasing as single dominant cause.

Single-component upgrade: add 2/3-rule dealiasing on the nonlinear products. Keep RK4, keep Nx=256, keep dt=1e-4. Do NOT also reduce dt or add filtering simultaneously — one change at a time per progressive-complexity discipline.

ic: u(x,0)=1.5*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2 (smoothed bore); v(x,0)=1.5*sech^2(x+8) (KdV soliton at x=-8)
method: Fourier pseudospectral + explicit RK4 + 2/3-rule dealiasing applied to all nonlinear products (u^2, v^2, u*v). Single-component upgrade from E1.
bank_use_rationale: NoKB — no bank; F1 attributed failure to aliasing from steep bore; the single textbook fix is 2/3-rule (Orszag); change only this component.

diagnostics.u_blowup_pattern: Gibbs oscillations on shock face grow monotonically from t=0.5; |u|>5 first at t=0.5, count 22 cells by t=8
diagnostics.interpretation: Dealiasing prevents NaN cascade and the soliton dynamics are clean, but with NO dissipation the bore — which is essentially a Burgers shock — develops unbounded Gibbs oscillations under pure spectral discretization. Soliton phenomenon target met (v peak >=0.5) but u target |u_max|<5 violated.

rationale: Soliton physics resolved correctly; bore needs minimal added dissipation to be bounded.

Single-component addition over E2: add low-amplitude hyperviscosity -eps*u_xxxx applied to u only (the variable with the shock). Choose eps small enough that low-k soliton modes are untouched. This is one more component on top of E2 (dealiasing kept).

ic: u(x,0)=1.5*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2 (smoothed bore); v(x,0)=1.5*sech^2(x+8) (KdV soliton at x=-8)
method: Pseudospectral + RK4 + 2/3 dealiasing + low-amplitude hyperviscosity -eps*u_xxxx on u only (eps=1e-5). One added component vs E2.

params.rationale_hyperviscosity_strength: At k=2/3*k_max=17.9, damping rate eps*k^4=1.02/s (kills Gibbs in ~1 time unit). At soliton-scale k~6, damping rate 0.015/s (timescale 64s) — soliton unaffected over T=8.

bank_use_rationale: NoKB — no bank; F2 attributed bore blow-up to absent dissipation; minimal-impact single-component addition is hyperviscosity. Strength chosen to be very weak at soliton scales (k~6) and strong at Gibbs scales (k~18).

diagnostics.u_target_meet: BORDERLINE FAIL — |u_max| exceeds 5 at most snaps; peak 8.54 at t=6.5; final t=8 value 6.33
diagnostics.interpretation: Hyperviscosity eps=1e-5 controls Gibbs but coupling forcing -d/dx(3 v^2 + v_xx) from soliton driving u through the bore amplifies u beyond 5. v target met (>=0.5 at every t), bore did not blow up (no NaN, mass conserved to 0.2%), soliton survived. Reducing the over-shoot would need stronger hyperviscosity (e.g. 5e-5) OR a longer effective bore width — both single-component changes that would constitute a 4th experiment (budget consumed).

rationale: Phenomenon target |u_max|<5 not fully met (peak 8.54, final 6.33), though soliton/bore qualitative behavior is correct. Budget of 3 experiments exhausted; reporting E3 as final answer with honest partial assessment.
Iteration budget (3) consumed. E3 is the best of E1 (NaN), E2 (u_max=22.9), E3 (u_max=8.5). Submitting E3.
